title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Execution Trace: Feed Go Origin Fairness
summary: The scheduler launches one goroutine per source, results block after cancellation, one origin occupies all workers, panics leak claims, and shutdown exits before commits settle. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go origin fairness increment in bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Prevent one publisher from consuming all slots while preserving useful throughput and explicit per-origin limits.
Assign producers, closers, receivers, buffers, and terminal states so sends, closes, and drains cannot race or block forever.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Origin Fairness · Theory Feed Go Origin Fairness
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6835 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
